title: Collapse a 5-branch cond into function heads with guards
prompt: Refactor MyApp.Pricing.tier_for/1 in test_fixtures/cond_role_router.ex into lib/my_app/pricing.ex. The current implementation is a 5-branch cond that returns a tier atom based on user attributes (spend, age_days, invited_by, churned?, trial_ends_at). Replace the cond with multiple def tier_for(user) clauses, each using a when guard to narrow. Guards must compose with and/or — use is_nil/1, Date comparisons via Date.compare/2 (in a function body if necessary; but prefer simpler guards). The order of function heads must reflect the priority of the original cond branches — most-specific first. Keep the same public signature and return values: :enterprise, :premium, :retention, :trial, :free.
tier_rationale: Requires recognizing that cond branches with simple predicates collapse to function-head guards, understanding the ordering semantics, and composing multi-predicate guards without reaching for Date/Enum functions that aren't allowed in guards. Sonnet typically keeps a case or collapses to fewer heads than needed.
